name: omarchy_mac_support
content:
Mac support 
As of Omarchy 3, there's buit-in support for Inte Macs. There are a coupe of known
imitations at the moment, but as ong as you're aware and OK with those; you can breathe
some new ife into your od Macs by oading Omarchy.
Pease note that instaing on an M-series Mac is not directy supported at this time. You
can find out more about the state of this in #omarchy-on-other in our Discord.
In a simpe test, we were abe to achieve 36% performance gains on a 2019 MacBook Pro
just by instaing Omarchy.
Instaing Omarchy on Mac 
Omarchy ony supports being the ony OS instaed at the moment. During the instaation,
the drive wi be wiped and MacOS wi no onger be bootabe.
You can sti restore it ater via Internet Recovery if you'd ike.
For the sake of this part, we' assume you've aready reviewed Getting Started and have
your USB drive ready. If you don't go ahead and do that now.
Disabe Secure Boot 
It is necessary to disabe Appe's Secure Boot in order to boot the bootabe USB, as we

---

It is necessary to disabe Appe's Secure Boot in order to boot the bootabe USB, as we
as the OS. To disabe it, perform the foowing:
�Turn off your Mac
�Turn it on and immediatey press and hod Command-R unti you see the oading
screen appear
�Seect your user and enter your password if prompted
�Once in the recovery screen, choose Utiities > Startup Security Utiity from the
menubar
�Enter your password when prompted to authenticate
�Choose "No Security" from the Secure Boot options
�Choose "Aow booting from externa or removabe media" from the Externa Boot
options
Start the Instaation 
�Insert the USB drive
�Restart your Mac and immediatey press and hod Option unti you see a screen of
boot devices
�Seect the orange EFI Boot device
�Proceed with the insta as norma
Known Limitations 
Members of the community are constanty working on soutions to these chaenges so if
these are probematic for you, join #omarchy-on-other in our Discord and see if there's
any up-to-date methods for resoving these.
Devices with T1 Chip 
The Appe T1 chip was introduced in ate 2016 and used excusivey in the first-generation
MacBook Pro modes with Touch Bar.
• MacBook Pro 13-inch 2016, two Thunderbot 3 ports) – Mode: A1706
• MacBook Pro 13-inch 2016, four Thunderbot 3 ports) – Mode: A1708
• MacBook Pro 15-inch 2016 – Mode: A1707
Known Issues 
• Touch Bar is non-functiona
• Sound is not functioning
Devices with T2 Chip 
The Appe T2 Security Chip was introduced in 2017. The T2 chip was discontinued with
the transition to Appe siicon M-series chips) starting in 2020.
iMac Pro 2017 – Mode: A1862
